ghr v0.8.0 improves GitHub workflow reliability around pull request creation, repo tab focus, release packaging, and comment/activity identity.

Highlights:

- Added Homebrew tap packaging docs and a staged `ghr-cli` formula, while keeping the installed executable name as `ghr`.
- Pull request creation now performs stronger preflight checks before calling `gh pr create`, including branch/commit validation and clearer modal feedback when something needs to be fixed first.
- PR creation from a local branch now pushes the branch before opening the pull request, avoiding GitHub errors caused by missing remote refs.
- GHR and Sections tab navigation now preserves focus while switching tabs, so `h`/`l` and `[`/`]` remain usable across repo and section changes.
- PR timeline commit activity now displays and links GitHub login names instead of commit author display names when GitHub does not provide an activity actor.
- macOS CI runs are more stable, and temporary config directories used by tests are isolated more reliably.
- Updated README release docs and pinned install examples for v0.8.0.

Install:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chenyukang/ghr/main/install.sh | sh
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chenyukang/ghr/main/install.ps1 | iex
```

Cargo:

```bash
cargo install ghr-cli
```
